Link-Serials (topologies)
Link-Serials — Topologies

Each Serial is a connection topology (origin ↔ destination). Frequencies / equipment depend on the Use Case context.

Use Cases (jump)
Legend
NU NATO Unclassified
NR NATO Restricted
NS NATO Secret
MS Mission Secret
Risk dots ●●●●● = LPI/LPD posture (more dots = lower probability of intercept/detection). Per AJP-3.6, applied per AJP-3.1.
References
• MC 195/12 — ATO Minimum Common Ship-Fitting Standards for RCE (extrapolated)
• AJP-3.1 Ed B — Allied Joint Doctrine for Maritime Operations
• AJP-3.6 — Allied Joint Doctrine for Electronic Warfare (LPI/LPD)
• AJP-6 Ed B — Allied Joint Doctrine for CIS
• AC/322-D(2024)0053 — NISP Baseline 16 (PFL-00404)
• STANAG 4711 — Standards Profile for FMN Spiral 4
• AP-06 NATO Glossary of Terms and Definitions • AP-15 NATO Glossary of AJPs
• ACP-176 NATO SUPP-2 — NATO Naval & Maritime Air Radio Org
• APP-7(D) Joint Brevity Words • APP-11 NATO MTF
• STANAG 5066/4538 (HF), 5511/5516/5522 (TDLs), 4193 (IFF M5), 4231 (UHF DAMA)
• STANAG 1074 (acoustic comms) • CSD buoys • AEHF XDR / NATO SATCOM POL/SHF (NSP4)
• STANAG 4586 (UAV) • Common Data Link (CDL) • IMO SOLAS Ch. IV (GMDSS)
